Add download page migrated from static HTML to Nuxt 3

Convert the static download/index.html page to a Vue SFC with:
- useHead() for SEO metadata (title, description, OG, Twitter, canonical)
- Reactive state (ref/computed) replacing all DOM manipulation

- Filter controls for app type, OS, and architecture

- Internal links converted to NuxtLink with absolute paths
- External links kept as regular <a> tags

commit.message:
fix(sync): warn when plugin has no effective clients and is skipped (#240)

* feat!: inline plugin skills config, workspace schema v2 (#233)

BREAKING CHANGE: skill selection is now configured inline on each
plugin entry instead of top-level disabledSkills/enabledSkills arrays.

- Add PluginSkillsConfig type: string[] (allowlist) or {exclude:string[]} (blocklist)
- Add skills field to PluginEntrySchema object variant
- Add version field to WorkspaceConfigSchema
- Update getAllSkillsFromPlugins and collectPluginSkills to read inline skills
- Add pluginSkillsMode to SkillInfo for caller convenience
- Update workspace-modify and user-workspace write functions to write inline
- Simplify plugin-skills CLI: remove inAllowlistMode detection, use SkillInfo.pluginSkillsMode
- Add migrateWorkspaceSkillsV1toV2 / migrateUserWorkspaceSkillsV1toV2 called on sync
- Bump to v1.0.0, add CHANGELOG.md

Migration: automatic on next workspace sync. No manual action required.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(security): eliminate script injection vulnerabilities in action.yml (#85)

* Initial plan

* fix(security): prevent script injection in action.yml

- Pass `inputs.atmos-config-path` through env var and quote it in
  `realpath` call to prevent command injection
- Pass `inputs.base-ref` and `inputs.skip-checkout` through env vars
  and use a bash array in the `git checkout` call to prevent injection
- Remove the `affected-args` intermediate step that built a raw shell
  string from unquoted `${{ inputs.* }}` expressions
- Replace `eval` in both `atmos describe affected` invocations with
  bash arrays built from env vars, eliminating arbitrary code execution
  via user-controlled inputs (`atmos-stack`, `identity`, etc.)

Co-authored-by: nitrocode <7775707+nitrocode@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(security): DRY up repeated args-building logic between affected steps

Extract shared argument construction into a dedicated 'Build atmos
affected command args' step that writes args (one per line) to
${RUNNER_TEMP}/atmos-affected-args. Both 'affected-pro' and 'affected'
steps then read them back with mapfile, passing only the step-specific
flag (--upload or --file=) inline. No eval, no injection surface.

Co-authored-by: nitrocode <7775707+nitrocode@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: nitrocode <7775707+nitrocode@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Andriy Knysh <aknysh@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add configurable hostname and admin user password (#187)

* feat(bootstrap): add configurable hostname and admin user password

- Add `hostname` and `admin_user_password` to config.toml template
- Fix /etc/hosts to properly map 127.0.0.1→localhost and 127.0.1.1→hostname
- Set admin user password via password_hash('sha512') for Cockpit login
- Require `hostname` in bootstrap playbook config validation

* docs: update documentation for hostname and admin_user_password config

Update bootstrap, config, and getting-started docs to reflect new
config.toml settings. Clarify admin_user_password is optional (for
Cockpit login) while hostname is required.

* fix(fail2ban): use systemd backend for SSH jails (#189)

* fix(fail2ban): use systemd backend for SSH jails instead of missing auth.log

* docs(fail2ban): document jail configuration and systemd backend usage

* docs update

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(ansible_user): decouple admin user creation from password setting

Admin user creation was gated on admin_user_password being set, which
skipped user creation entirely when password was empty. Split into two
tasks: always create user, conditionally set password. Also use
update_password: on_create for idempotent reruns.

* fix(bootstrap): tighten /etc/hosts regex to avoid prefix matching

^127\.0\.0\.1 also matches 127.0.0.10. Add \s after the IP to ensure
only the exact address followed by whitespace is matched.

* test(ansible_runner): assert hostname in bootstrap required keys

* docs: clarify admin_user_password is optional across all docs

Mark admin_user_password as optional (only needed for Cockpit login)
in first-deployment, bootstrap, secrets, and cockpit documentation.

* docs: clarify optional password and fallback in layers and cockpit docs

* fix(bootstrap): add hostname validation before VPS connection

* fix(ansible_user): add no_log to password task to prevent secret leakage

* chore: sync config.example.toml with TEMPLATE in user_config.rs

Adds missing keys: hostname, admin_user_password, bichon_*,
headscale_subdomain, paperless_subdomain, paperless_tailscale_ip,
restic_*, tailscale_login_server, and vdirsyncer_*.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: include hardening.yml in tag resolution (#192)

* fix(fail2ban): use systemd backend for SSH jails (#189)

* fix(fail2ban): use systemd backend for SSH jails instead of missing auth.log

* docs(fail2ban): document jail configuration and systemd backend usage

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(cli): include hardening.yml in tag resolution

The dependency resolver only scanned infrastructure.yml and apps.yml,
making `auberge ansible run --tags fail2ban` unresolvable.

Add hardening.yml to target_playbooks and PLAYBOOK_ORDER, and add role
names (fail2ban, kernel_hardening) as tags for consistency with other
playbooks.

Closes #191

* refactor(cli): address review feedback on tag resolution

- Remove duplicate target_playbooks array, reuse PLAYBOOK_ORDER as
  single source of truth
- Rename misleading test to test_resolve_fail2ban_tag_targets_hardening

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: revise all READMEs and add consolidated PDF user guide

- Rewrite root README.md: updated directory tree, pipeline descriptions,
  removed all ep1_ibc_ibt_analysis references, highlighted
  ep_structure_analysis as current scientific focus, corrected
  data source URLs and setup instructions
- Rewrite scripts/README.md: accurate subdirectory descriptions,
  ep_structure_analysis scientific summary, corrected main/ description
  as final publication figure scripts (not 'main analyses')
- Update scripts/main/README.md: remove ep1_ibc_ibt_analysis dependency
  references from S2/S3 figures
- Update scripts/ck_subterms_analysis/README.md: fix prerequisite to
  reference cluster results (not ep1_ibc_ibt_analysis), correct EP1
  count to 444 cyclones
- Rewrite scripts/preprocess_data/README.md: accurate script list,
  correct output paths, proper run order
- Create scripts/exploratory/README.md: new README listing all 15
  exploratory scripts with purpose, inputs, and outputs
- Create scripts/documentation/compile_docs.py: reproducible script
  that collects 9 READMEs, generates combined Markdown, and compiles
  to docs/user_guide_repository_readmes.pdf via pandoc
- Add docs/user_guide_combined.md and docs/user_guide_repository_readmes.pdf

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: automate Cloudflare DNS record creation in Ansible roles (#181)

* Initial plan

* feat: automate Cloudflare DNS record creation in Ansible roles

Co-authored-by: sripwoud <38692952+sripwoud@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(dns_record): add input validation, error handling, and zone ID caching

- Assert required variables upfront for clear failure messages
- Add failed_when checking Cloudflare API success field to catch
  logical failures returned with HTTP 200
- Add changed_when for accurate idempotency reporting
- Use verbosity-gated no_log so errors are visible with -vvv
- Cache zone ID across multiple role inclusions in same play
- Add meta/main.yml matching project conventions

* docs: update DNS documentation for automatic provisioning

DNS A records are now auto-provisioned during app deployment via the
dns_record role. Update first-deployment guide, managing-records,
batch-operations, cloudflare-setup, infrastructure-layers, ansible-roles
guide, and troubleshooting docs to reflect this change.

* feat(cockpit): add dns_record integration with Tailscale IP

Cockpit binds to the Tailscale interface, so its DNS A record must
point to the Tailscale IP rather than the public VPS IP.

* fix(dns_record): validate Cloudflare success flag on all API calls

- Add failed_when on zone lookup and record lookup GET requests
- Check for exactly 1 zone result instead of just non-empty
- Use verbosity-gated no_log on assert task

* fix(dns_record): domain-scoped cache, IPv4 validation, drift detection

- Cache zone IDs in a dict keyed by domain (safe for multi-domain plays)
- Validate dns_record_ip matches IPv4 format in assert block
- Fail explicitly when multiple A records found for same FQDN
- Update task now triggers on IP, TTL, or proxied changes (not just IP)

* docs: add cockpit to integrated apps lists, fix troubleshooting causes

- Include cockpit in all dns_record integration app lists
- Broaden batch-operations example to include all non-integrated apps
- Rewrite troubleshooting causes to reflect realistic failure scenarios

* fix(dns_record): strict IPv4 octet-range validation, update docs example

- Tighten regex to reject out-of-range octets (e.g. 999.999.999.999)
- Update managing-records example to show only non-integrated apps

* fix(dns_record): prevent token leakage, use idiomatic regex test

- Keep no_log: true on all URI tasks to prevent Bearer token exposure
  in CI logs or shared terminals at any verbosity level
- Surface Cloudflare API errors via separate fail tasks that log only
  sanitized json.errors field
- Switch is regex() to is match() for Ansible idiom consistency

* fix(dns_record): include HTTP status and msg fallback in error messages

All fail tasks now surface status code and Ansible's msg field when
Cloudflare json.errors is unavailable (timeouts, TLS errors, non-JSON
responses).

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: sripwoud <38692952+sripwoud@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: sripwoud <me@sripwoud.xyz>

commit.message:
fix: prevent grouping consecutive code blocks with the same language (#667)

* fix: prevent grouping consecutive code blocks with the same language

Two consecutive code blocks with the same language (e.g., both `mjs`)
were incorrectly grouped into a CJS/MJS switchable tab. Add a check
to ensure the two code blocks have different languages before creating
a switchable code tab.

Refs: https://github.com/nodejs/node/pull/62181

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add optional chaining for defensive null safety

* fix: update to assume [0] and [1] always exist

---------

commit.message:
docs(house-forecast): finalize increment 6 docs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix falsy enum values returning None instead of name

AlarmReason.NO_ALARM has value 0, making it falsy in Python. The
_enum_to_lowercase function used 'if enum_value' which incorrectly
returned None for NO_ALARM. Changed to 'if enum_value is not None'
to properly handle zero-valued Flag enums.

This caused battery_monitor alarm and dc_energy_meter alarm to show
as 'unknown' instead of 'no_alarm' in Home Assistant.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: victron-ble-ha-parser

commit.message:
feat: deploy `Cockpit` for web-based monitoring and control (#180)

* Initial plan

* feat: deploy Cockpit for web-based monitoring and control

Co-authored-by: sripwoud <38692952+sripwoud@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(cockpit): maintain alphabetical role ordering in apps playbook

* docs(cockpit): trim verbose sections and fix misleading config.toml reference

Remove inaccurate claim that cockpit variables are overridable via
config.toml — they are standard Ansible role defaults. Remove redundant
Features and Security sections (covered by the intro and Access section).

* docs(cockpit): add to applications overview table

* feat(cockpit): proxy through Caddy with real TLS instead of direct port access

Replace direct port 9090 access (self-signed cert + UFW rule) with the
same Caddy reverse proxy pattern used by paperless:
- Bind cockpit.socket to localhost only via systemd drop-in override
- AllowUnencrypted in cockpit.conf so Caddy handles all TLS
- Caddy Caddyfile bound to Tailscale IP with DNS-01 cert
- Remove UFW rule (no longer needed with localhost binding)

* docs(cockpit): update access docs for Caddy proxy pattern

* fix(cockpit): add Debian distribution guard for backports repo

---------

commit.message:
Add AC charger (smart charger) support

Add support for AcCharger device type (BLE record type 0x08), which
covers Blue Smart IP22/IP65, Phoenix Smart IP43, and other Victron
AC charger models.

New sensor keys: AC_CURRENT, OUTPUT_VOLTAGE_1-3, OUTPUT_CURRENT_1-3.
Existing keys reused: CHARGE_STATE, CHARGER_ERROR, TEMPERATURE.

Also adds snapshot tests for all 8 supported device types.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
@wordpress/ui: add Collapsible component (#76280)

* Add Collapsible component to @wordpress/ui

Thin wrapper around Base UI's Collapsible component with Root,
Trigger, and Panel subcomponents. No custom styles — this is a
pure pass-through of the Base UI API.

Made-with: Cursor

* Add Storybook stories for Collapsible

Covers default, default-open, disabled, and controlled variants.

Made-with: Cursor

* Add unit tests for Collapsible

Covers ref forwarding, uncontrolled/controlled open state,
disabled behavior, and className pass-through.

Made-with: Cursor

* Update CHANGELOG for Collapsible component

Made-with: Cursor

* Address review feedback

- Fix import ordering: external (@base-ui) before @wordpress
- Add render prop tests for Root, Trigger, and Panel

Made-with: Cursor

* Update CollapsibleCard to use internal Collapsible wrapper

Replace direct @base-ui/react/collapsible imports with the new
internal Collapsible component in all three CollapsibleCard files.

In header.tsx, derive the open state from the aria-expanded prop
instead of the Base UI state callback, since our wrapper's render
prop type uses a single-argument signature.

Made-with: Cursor

* Apply suggestion from @Copilot

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add comment explaining aria-expanded tradeoff in header render prop

The Collapsible wrapper's ComponentProps utility narrows the render
callback to a single-argument signature, so Base UI's `state` object
isn't available. Document why we read open state from `aria-expanded`.

Made-with: Cursor

* avoid unnecessary destructuring

* add hidden until found example

---------

Co-authored-by: ciampo <mciampini@git.wordpress.org>
Co-authored-by: mirka <0mirka00@git.wordpress.org>

commit.message:
ci: add checks:write permission for test-reporter

dorny/test-reporter@v1 requires checks:write permission to create
check runs. Without it, the action fails with 'Resource not accessible
by integration' on pull requests.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Convert C++/Qt DFT visualizer to Rust/Dioxus 0.7.3 (#2)

commit.message:
Update to .NET 10 SDK with workload version set pinning (#3514)

Upgrade SkiaSharp from .NET 8 SDK to .NET 10 SDK with comprehensive build system,
CI pipeline, TFM architecture, and workload management changes.

TFM Architecture:
- Restructured from Previous/Current/Next to Base/Previous/Current
- TFMBase=net6.0 (class libraries), TFMPrevious=net9.0, TFMCurrent=net10.0
- Dropped TFMNext (platform TFMs only support 2 versions at once)
- Added Previous TPVs for all platforms alongside Current TPVs

SDK & Tooling:
- global.json: SDK 8.0.100 -> 10.0.100, rollForward latestMinor -> latestFeature
- latestFeature allows any 10.0.xxx feature band across heterogeneous CI agents
- .NET SDK pipeline variable: 8.0.304 -> 10.0.103
- Xcode: 15.4 -> 26.2 (managed), 15.4 -> 16.2 (native)
- Docker images: dotnet/sdk:8.0 -> dotnet/sdk:10.0
- Emscripten: 3.1.34 -> 3.1.56
- macOS agents: macos-14 -> macos-15
- Android tests moved from macOS to Linux agents

Workload Management:
- Replaced ad-hoc manifest mode with deterministic workload version set pinning
- Official workloads install with 'dotnet workload install --version 10.0.104'
- Tizen installs separately (not part of official workload sets)
- Removed DOTNET_VERSION_PREVIEW, DOTNET_WORKLOAD_SOURCE, installPreviewVs
- Removed manifest mode workarounds (--skip-manifest-update)

Platform Versions (Previous / Current):
- Android: 35.0 / 36.0
- iOS: 18.0 / 26.2
- Mac Catalyst: 18.0 / 26.2
- tvOS: 18.0 / 26.2
- macOS: 15.0 / 26.2
- Tizen: 8.0 / 10.0

Package Updates:
- xunit: 2.4.2 -> 2.9.3
- xharness CLI: 9.0.0-prerelease.24317.3 -> 11.0.0-prerelease.26107.1
- DeviceRunners: 0.1.0-preview.1 -> 0.1.0-preview.6
- TypeScript MSBuild: 5.7.1 -> 5.9.3
- Uno.Sdk: 5.2.175 -> 6.5.31

Blazor TypeScript Fix:
- Replaced TypeScriptCompileBlocked with per-TFM TypeScriptOutDir to fix
   multi-TFM parallel build race condition on shared wwwroot/*.js files

CI Cleanup:
- Removed old NuGet feeds (dotnet6/7/8, test-device-runners) from nuget.config
- Fixed Android emulator XDG path mismatch on Linux via ANDROID_AVD_HOME
- Added ANDROID_PLATFORM_VERSIONS: 21,35,36

Samples:
- MAUI: Removed Tizen platform folder, removed UseMauiCompatibility
- Uno: Updated to 6.5, migrated to UnoPlatformHostBuilder
- All: Updated TFMs and SupportedOSPlatformVersion minimums

Documentation:
- New: documentation/updating-dotnet-version.md for future upgrades

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: SkiaSharp
repository.description: SkiaSharp is a cross-platform 2D graphics API for .NET platforms based on Google's Skia Graphics Library. It provides a comprehensive 2D API that can be used across mobile, server and desktop models to render images.
